messages.content:
CVE-2020-16038 is a high-severity vulnerability identified as a "use-after-free" condition (CWE-416) in the media component of Google Chrome on OS X versions prior to 87.0.4280.88. This flaw occurs when memory is referenced after it has been freed, potentially leading to program crashes, the use of unexpected values, or even arbitrary code execution.

The vulnerability allows a remote attacker to exploit heap corruption via a crafted HTML page, posing significant security risks such as unauthorized access or control of affected systems. The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique is T1203, which involves exploiting software vulnerabilities.

Users and administrators are advised to update affected Google Chrome installations to version 87.0.4280.88 or later, as this release addresses the vulnerability. Employ additional mitigation techniques like M1050 (Hardening), M1051 (Vulnerability Response), and M1048 (Application Isolation) to enhance security and reduce the attack surface.
